{"society_power": 7.7, "state_power": 7.3, "society_change": 0.3, "state_change": 0.1, "key_event": "Liberalization, IT boom, PIL/RTI, and coalition-era societal mobilization", "reasoning": "Deepening economic reforms, a booming IT/services middle class, judicial activism (PIL), grassroots transparency movements, and panchayati raj devolution meaningfully strengthened society, while the 2004 electoral turnover showed real accountability. The state saw offsetting forces\u2014Pokhran-II, Kargil, and stable NDA coalition boosted coercive and executive capacity, but reduced dirigiste economic control and the 2002 Gujarat failure of protection left central state power roughly flat."}
